description: subdelegation off v01 grants `lock:seal(recipient=bc1qmallory…)` — bc1qmallory is NOT bc1qalice, and v01's only scope binds recipient to bc1qalice with op `=`. Per SPEC §7.4 sub-scope rules, an `=` constraint admits only the same value; this is scope escalation. Verifier MUST reject with E_SUBDELEGATION_SCOPE_ESCALATED per SUB-DELEGATION.md §2.2 step 3g.

expected.rejection_reason: v01 grants lock:seal(recipient=bc1qalice…). The exercised sub-delegation's scope lock:seal(recipient=bc1qmallory…) has the same key/op (`recipient=`) but a different value, so it is NOT a sub-scope of v01's grant. The chain link fails containment.

harness_assertion: Independent of canonical-message hashing or BIP-322 signature validity, the chain verifier returns E_SUBDELEGATION_SCOPE_ESCALATED at step 3g. Implementations that test scope containment first (before signature verification) MAY reach this verdict without a BIP-322 check; either ordering is conformant per the listed step order.
